title: Spatially variable gene detection
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"19eq","text":"Spatially variable gene detection from Kilfeather, Khoo et al., 2024","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Spatially variable genes were identified using SpatialDE2 with default settings. Coordinates and full expression matrices of each brain were supplied, divided by cell type. To combine results from separate samples, a meta-analysis was performed on the raw P values from each sample, using Fisher’s method. The Benjamini & Hochberg correction for multiple comparisons was subsequently used and genes were considered spatially variable with an adjusted P value of < 0.01.
creator: Peter Kilfeather
